title: abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus - Zero-server client-side code intelligence with multi-IDE MCP + SWE-bench eval harness
summary: 27K-star TypeScript monorepo shipping a zero-server, client-side code intelligence tool with a graph-backed MCP server reaching 5 IDEs (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Windsurf, OpenCode). Most valuable to SoNash NOT as a product but as reference implementations of: three-mode SWE-bench eval harness, gitnexus:start MCP contract block format, marketplace.json plugin distribution, and PreToolUse ambient enrichment pattern.
creator_view: See creator-view.md. Central insight: GitNexus shifts cognitive load from LLM to indexing pipeline (precomputed clusters, flows, confidence scores) - tools return complete context in one call. The challenge for SoNash is not to adopt code intel but to adopt the eval-harness discipline: 450+ patterns and 77 skills need measurement apparatus (baseline vs enriched mode, fixed benchmark, per-instance metrics, commit-hash caching). Marketplace.json validates Wave 5 Rank 3 as real and shippable. Signs pattern in GUARDRAILS.md is MEMORY.md feedback entries codified at repo level.

candidates.name: SWE-bench 3-mode eval harness shape
candidates.description: Baseline / native / native_augment modes, per-instance cached by (repo, commit), multi-model support. Structural pattern for measuring agent capability gain from meta-tooling.

candidates.name: gitnexus:start MCP contract block
candidates.description: Structured Always/When/Never block in CLAUDE.md defining MCP tool usage rules with impact risk levels (d=1/d=2/d=3). Template for SoNash when it emits MCP tool contracts.

candidates.name: Signs pattern (recurring failures as codified rules)
candidates.description: GUARDRAILS.md introduces 'Signs' - recurring failure patterns codified once observed twice. Analogous to SoNash MEMORY.md feedback entries but repo-visible.

candidates.name: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json reference impl
candidates.description: Canonical format for Claude Code marketplace: marketplace.json + plugin.json (name, version, source, description). Wave 5 Rank 3 validation.

candidates.name: Typed DAG phase pipeline
candidates.description: 12-phase ingestion DAG with typed deps[], execute(ctx, deps), and output. Cycles validated at startup. Adding a phase is a new file + buildPhaseList() registration. Relevant to plan-orchestration Wave 2+ (SWS CANON).

candidates.name: Multi-IDE MCP negotiation at install time
candidates.description: One MCP server reaches 5 IDEs. Setup command auto-detects editor and writes config once. Claude Code gets PreToolUse+PostToolUse depth; others get base tools.

candidates.name: PreToolUse ambient grep enrichment
candidates.description: Hook intercepts grep results and appends graph context (callers, callees, processes) silently. Makes meta-tooling ambient rather than explicit.

candidates.name: Confidence-scored edges
candidates.description: Every relation carries confidence 0.7-1.0 (exact/heuristic/fallback/lenient). Tools expose minConfidence filter. Lets agents decide when to clarify vs proceed.

candidates.name: Precomputed relational intelligence
candidates.description: Shift cognitive load from LLM to indexing pipeline: precompute clusters (Leiden), flows, confidence. Tools return complete context in one call. Indirect relevance to /recall improvements.

candidates.name: superpowers plan format (TDD-first executable spec)
candidates.description: Structured plan: write failing test - verify fail - implement - verify pass - commit template. Checkbox tracking per step, spec ref. Real-world example at docs/superpowers/plans/.

candidates.name: SWE-bench eval server daemon
candidates.description: HTTP daemon (localhost:5959) ~50x faster than cold CLI per tool call. Only relevant if SoNash measures skill invocation cost.

candidates.name: eval/README.md (SWE-bench harness ref)
candidates.description: 214-line reference. 3 modes, metrics, caching, multi-model support.

candidates.name: CLAUDE.md gitnexus:start contract block
candidates.description: The template block for MCP tool contracts in CLAUDE.md.

candidates.name: AGENTS.md canonical rules + gitnexus:start
candidates.description: Canonical agent rules + MCP block.

candidates.name: GUARDRAILS.md with Signs pattern
candidates.description: Non-negotiables + Signs (recurring failures codified as rules).

candidates.name: gitnexus-pr-review skill (graph-impact-based PR review)
candidates.description: PR review via graph impact d=1/2/3 classification. Complements SoNash /pr-review (bot feedback processing).

candidates.name: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
candidates.description: Canonical marketplace.json format.

candidates.name: docs/superpowers/plans/pr626-high-fixes.md
candidates.description: 725-line TDD-first superpowers plan - real-world example.

candidates.name: MCP tool shadowing (naming conflicts with native tools)
candidates.description: Defining MCP tools with names that conflict with native tools (e.g., custom `grep`). GitNexus noodlbox-comparison draft flags this as unsafe. Lesson: name SoNash MCP tools distinctly (sonash_search_patterns, not search).

candidates.name: Aspirational docs outrunning implementation
candidates.description: swift-ingestion-gaps.md, type-resolution-roadmap.md at root alongside shipped docs. GitNexus mitigates by using .sisyphus/drafts/ for clearly-marked drafts. Lesson: never let brainstorm/plan content leak into shipped docs.

candidates.name: Over-featuring before measurement
candidates.description: GitNexus has ~1 core capability + 1 eval harness. SoNash has 77 skills + 38 agents + 450 patterns + 0 eval harness. Inverse ratio. Lesson: add eval before skill N+1.

candidates.name: Graph/schema versioning without migration path
candidates.description: GitNexus graph schema has no documented migration path. Same risk applies to SoNash SQLite index at .research/content-analysis.db. Lesson: add MIGRATIONS.md when schema is next touched.

absence_patterns.note: Roadmapped but not shipped - every example assumes full re-index
Multi-tenant, audit logging, secret management vestigial
Uses hybrid BM25+semantic+RRF but embedder choice, drift management silent
No documented migration path when graph schema changes
User code flows into LLM prompts unescaped (S2 security)

related_repos.reason: Potential overlap on eval harness patterns
Both emit Claude Code integration via MCP, different domain

cross_repo_connections.detail: Both care about measuring agent capability; GitNexus ships SWE-bench harness, autoresearch has research agent methodology
Both use MCP as the integration contract with agent environments
Explicit competitor per .sisyphus/drafts/noodlbox-comparison.md. Not yet analyzed. Session Hooks pattern identified as steal-worthy.

adoption_blockers.note: License is NOASSERTION. Must be clarified before any production use. Check LICENSE file or file issue.
3 S2 findings: unguarded LLM prompt injection, GITHUB_TOKEN env-inheritance in subprocess, no dep scanning. Trial on non-sensitive branch first.
Every commit forces full re-index. Tolerable now; worsens with repo growth.
gitnexus:start MCP block prescribes MUST/NEVER rules (e.g., impact-before-edit). Adopting changes agent discipline.
akonlabs.com referenced as enterprise/SaaS but community vs commercial feature split not documented.

adoption_recommendation.step_1_license_check: E0 - Check LICENSE file or file issue. If MIT/Apache, proceed. If proprietary, stop and extract ideas only.
adoption_recommendation.step_2_trial: E1 - Install on throwaway SoNash branch. Run `npx gitnexus analyze` + `gitnexus setup`. Use for one real task (PR review or refactor).
adoption_recommendation.step_3_verdict: E0 - Either adopt as standard tooling + add sonash:start contract block, or uninstall and extract patterns only.

adoption_gain_day_one: gitnexus_impact — d=1/d=2/d=3 blast radius before editing any of SoNash's 77 skills or 38 agents
gitnexus_query — concept-based search over process-grouped results (alternative to grep)
PostToolUse hook re-indexes after every commit (automatic freshness)
Works with existing Claude Code; zero SoNash code changes required
